why_this_source: dak is the root table for THIRD_PARTY_PAYMENTS (fk_section IN (142, 228, 265, 383)) and contains primary identity, amount, status, and chronology. It drives downstream flow and is enriched with runtime workflow features for flow integrity checks.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_dakid_no
feature_plan.reason: DAK identifier is required for traceability; missing DAKID indicates invalid or unregistered payment entry.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_list_no
feature_plan.reason: List number is required for DAK traceability; its absence may indicate incomplete or forged entry.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_reference_no
feature_plan.reason: Reference number from vendor/authority is a key alternate identifier; missing value may indicate fake or unverified source.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_bill_no
feature_plan.reason: Bill number is required for third-party payments; missing value suggests unverified or fabricated bill.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_bill_date
feature_plan.reason: Bill date is required; missing date breaks chronology and may indicate manipulation.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_list_date
feature_plan.reason: List date is required for DAK chronology; missing value undermines audit trail.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_reference_date
feature_plan.reason: Reference date is required for document evidence; missing value weakens authenticity.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_amount
feature_plan.reason: Amount is required for payment validation; missing value indicates incomplete or invalid entry.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_month_year
feature_plan.reason: Month/year context is required for accounting and approval power slab; missing value breaks fiscal traceability.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_record_status
feature_plan.reason: Record status is required for validity; missing status prevents flow and approval tracking.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_created_at
feature_plan.reason: Creation timestamp is required for workflow chronology; missing value breaks recency and audit trail.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_fk_section
feature_plan.reason: Section ID is required to filter only THIRD_PARTY_PAYMENTS; missing value indicates invalid scope.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_fk_central_unit
feature_plan.reason: Central unit reference is required for unit-level accountability; missing value breaks traceability.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_subject
feature_plan.reason: Subject is required for payment purpose context; missing value weakens narrative traceability.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_bill_date_when_bill_no
feature_plan.reason: When bill number exists, bill date must be present; missing date breaks chronology.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_reference_date_when_reference_no
feature_plan.reason: When reference number exists, reference date must be present; missing date breaks evidence chain.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_list_date_when_list_no
feature_plan.reason: When list number exists, list date must be present; missing date breaks DAK chronology.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_dakid_no_when_record_status_valid
feature_plan.reason: Valid DAK records (record_status='V') must have a DAKID; missing DAKID indicates invalid entry.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_bill_no_when_record_status_valid
feature_plan.reason: Valid DAK records must have a bill number; missing bill number indicates invalid entry.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_amount_when_record_status_valid
feature_plan.reason: Valid DAK records must have an amount; missing amount indicates invalid entry.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_month_year_when_record_status_valid
feature_plan.reason: Valid DAK records must have month/year context; missing value breaks fiscal traceability.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_fk_central_unit_when_record_status_valid
feature_plan.reason: Valid DAK records must have a central unit; missing unit breaks accountability.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_subject_when_record_status_valid
feature_plan.reason: Valid DAK records must have a subject; missing subject breaks purpose context.

why_this_source: The bill table is the central transactional record for third-party payments, containing all critical financial, identity, and workflow metadata. It links to DAK and downstream payment tables via FKs, and its validation rules are fully defined in the context. It is the primary source for detecting an

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_invoice_date_after_bill_date
feature_plan.reason: Invoice date must not be after bill creation date. This violates the chronological order of document submission and may indicate backdating.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_gst_applicable_without_tax_recovery
feature_plan.reason: If GST is applicable, recoveries must be present. Missing recoveries for GST-applicable bills may indicate tax evasion or incomplete payment calculation.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_duplicate_normalized_invoice
feature_plan.reason: Same invoice identity with different formatting (e.g., case, spaces) across records may indicate attempted evasion of duplicate checks.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_high_amount_ratio_to_group_median
feature_plan.reason: Amount passed significantly higher than median for the same vendor may indicate overpayment, unusual billing, or split payment evasion.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_low_amount_ratio_to_group_median
feature_plan.reason: Amount passed significantly lower than median for the same vendor may indicate underpayment, bill splitting, or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_high_amount_rank_pct_in_vendor_group
feature_plan.reason: A bill with a high percentile rank in amount within its vendor group may indicate outlier behavior or potential overpayment.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_low_amount_rank_pct_in_vendor_group
feature_plan.reason: A bill with a low percentile rank in amount within its vendor group may indicate underpayment or bill splitting.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_duplicate_invoice_in_nearby_dates
feature_plan.reason: Same invoice number from same vendor appearing within 7 days suggests possible bill splitting or repeated submission to bypass controls.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_high_rolling_window_amount_sum
feature_plan.reason: High cumulative amount for same invoice/vendor within 7 days may indicate attempted evasion of payment caps or split payment.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_no_downstream_payment_trail
feature_plan.reason: A bill with a valid DAK but no downstream payment records (cheque_slip, schedule3, ecs) suggests a broken payment flow or fake payment attempt.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_gst_applicable_and_no_recoveries
feature_plan.reason: GST-applicable bills must have recoveries. Missing recoveries may indicate tax evasion or incomplete payment calculation.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_duplicate_invoice_in_different_units
feature_plan.reason: Same invoice from same vendor appearing in different units may indicate attempted evasion of unit-level controls or duplicate payment.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_duplicate_invoice_in_different_daks
feature_plan.reason: Same invoice from same vendor appearing in different DAKs may indicate duplicate or split payment attempts.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_duplicate_invoice_in_different_payment_modes
feature_plan.reason: Same invoice from same vendor in different payment modes may indicate manipulation or split payment to bypass controls.

feature_plan.name: is_bill_with_duplicate_invoice_in_different_dates
feature_plan.reason: Same invoice from same vendor with different dates may indicate backdating or repeated submission.

why_this_source: cheque_slip is the final payment stage in the third-party payment flow; it contains the actual payment amount, beneficiary, vendor, bank, payment mode, approval status, and date. It is the most reliable source for detecting anomalies in payment execution, especially when compared to upstream bill an

feature_plan.name: is_missing_dak_link
feature_plan.reason: A missing fk_dak indicates the cheque slip is not linked to any valid DAK, which breaks the payment flow and suggests a fabricated or orphaned payment.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_vendor
feature_plan.reason: A missing vendor in a cheque slip implies the payment is made without a valid supplier identity, which is a red flag for fake or unverified payments.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_beneficiary
feature_plan.reason: A missing beneficiary indicates the payment lacks a defined recipient, which is suspicious in third-party payments where beneficiary identity is mandatory.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_favour_bank
feature_plan.reason: A missing favour bank suggests the payment lacks a defined financial institution, which is critical for traceability and fraud detection.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_payment_mode
feature_plan.reason: A missing payment mode indicates the payment type (e.g., ECS, cheque) is undefined, which breaks the payment flow and may indicate manipulation.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_payment_detail
feature_plan.reason: A missing payment detail reduces auditability and may indicate a lack of justification for the payment, especially if it should be derived from bill context.

feature_plan.name: is_not_approved
feature_plan.reason: Cheque slips that are not approved should not be processed; this flag detects unapproved payments that may have bypassed control.

feature_plan.name: is_not_valid_record_status
feature_plan.reason: Only record_status 'V' (valid) should be processed; any other status indicates invalid or rejected payment slips.

feature_plan.name: cheque_slip_date_in_future
feature_plan.reason: A future cheque slip date indicates a fabricated or incorrectly dated payment, which violates normal payment chronology.

feature_plan.name: duplicate_vendor_cheque_slip
feature_plan.reason: Multiple cheque slips for the same vendor may indicate bulk or split payments, which could be used to evade thresholds or detection.

feature_plan.name: duplicate_beneficiary_cheque_slip
feature_plan.reason: Multiple cheque slips to the same beneficiary may indicate repeated payments or payment splitting.

feature_plan.name: duplicate_payment_mode_cheque_slip
feature_plan.reason: Multiple cheque slips with the same payment mode may indicate batch processing, but if inconsistent with vendor or amount, may suggest manipulation.

feature_plan.name: duplicate_normalized_payment_detail
feature_plan.reason: Normalized duplicate payment details (ignoring case, spaces, punctuation) may indicate repeated or templated payment narratives, which could be used to mask fraud.

feature_plan.name: duplicate_vendor_payment_detail
feature_plan.reason: Same vendor with different payment details may indicate inconsistent or fabricated payment justifications.

feature_plan.name: duplicate_beneficiary_payment_detail
feature_plan.reason: Same beneficiary with different payment details may indicate inconsistent or fabricated payment justifications.

feature_plan.name: rolling_window_vendor_payment_count_7d
feature_plan.reason: High frequency of payments to the same vendor within 7 days may indicate bill splitting or repeated payments to evade scrutiny.

feature_plan.name: rolling_window_vendor_payment_amount_sum_7d
feature_plan.reason: High total payment amount to the same vendor within 7 days may indicate bill splitting or excessive payments.

feature_plan.name: ratio_to_group_median_vendor_amount
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount significantly higher or lower than the median for the same vendor may indicate overpayment, underpayment, or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: group_rank_pct_vendor_amount
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount at extreme percentile within the vendor’s history may indicate abnormal behavior.

feature_plan.name: group_frequency_vendor
feature_plan.reason: High frequency of payments to a vendor may indicate a pattern of repeated or bulk payments, which could be suspicious if not justified.

feature_plan.name: starts_with_fis_29
feature_plan.reason: FIS document numbers starting with '29' indicate GEM online transactions, which are not valid for normal third-party payments and should not be processed as such.

why_this_source: punching_medium is a core payment flow table with timestamped, amount-bearing, status-controlled records that link directly to dak. It captures the final accounting event and payment routing context, making it ideal for detecting flow breaks, timing anomalies, and payment trail weaknesses in third-p

feature_plan.name: is_missing_dak_link
feature_plan.reason: A punching medium row without a valid fk_dak indicates a broken upstream link, suggesting a payment record not tied to a legitimate DAK, which is a critical forensic red flag in third-party payment flows.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_pm_date
feature_plan.reason: Missing pm_date in a punching medium record breaks the chronological traceability of the payment event, undermining auditability and increasing risk of fraudulent or unverified transactions.

feature_plan.name: is_missing_sign_rc
feature_plan.reason: Missing sign_rc prevents aggregation of valid charged amounts; only '+C' rows should be included in payment totals, so missing sign_rc may indicate invalid or unaccounted entries.

feature_plan.name: is_invalid_sign_rc
feature_plan.reason: A sign_rc of '+D' indicates a credit entry, which is invalid in the context of a third-party payment debit. Such entries should not be included in payment flow and may indicate manipulation or reversal.

feature_plan.name: is_neft_generated_but_no_dpsheet
feature_plan.reason: If NEFT is generated but no DPSheet is created, it suggests a payment was initiated without proper internal documentation, indicating a potential bypass of internal controls.

feature_plan.name: is_dpsheet_generated_but_no_neft
feature_plan.reason: A DPSheet generated without NEFT indicates a payment was documented but not executed, which may signal a failed or delayed payment, or a potential ghost payment attempt.

feature_plan.name: is_cancelled_with_payment
feature_plan.reason: Cancelled punching medium records should not be active (record_status='A'). If a record is active but marked as cancelled, it indicates a status contradiction and potential manipulation of payment state.

feature_plan.name: is_active_with_no_payment_trail
feature_plan.reason: Active records (record_status='A') without NEFT generation suggest a payment was approved but not executed, which may indicate a stalled or fraudulent payment process.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_in_future
feature_plan.reason: A future pm_date indicates a payment scheduled for a date in the future, which may be valid, but when combined with active status and no NEFT, it raises suspicion of delayed or fake payment planning.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_no_dak
feature_plan.reason: Active payment records without a linked DAK are suspicious, as they lack a source document and cannot be traced to a valid claim or approval.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_duplicate_dak
feature_plan.reason: Multiple punching medium records linked to the same DAK may indicate duplicate payment attempts or splitting of a single payment across multiple entries, a common sign of bill splitting or fraud.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_duplicate_normalized_cmp_batch
feature_plan.reason: Duplicate CMP batch identifiers across different punching medium records may indicate batch-level manipulation or reuse of payment batches, which is a red flag for systemic fraud.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_duplicate_normalized_upload_batch
feature_plan.reason: Reused upload batches may indicate data reprocessing or duplication, which can mask fraudulent or duplicate payment entries.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_duplicate_normalized_ncs_transaction_id
feature_plan.reason: Duplicate NCS transaction IDs across different punching medium records suggest possible transaction reuse or manipulation, which is a serious forensic red flag.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_duplicate_normalized_detail_code_head
feature_plan.reason: Repeated detail_code_head values across different payments may indicate a pattern of reusing accounting codes, which could be used to mask improper or unauthorized expenditures.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_duplicate_distinct_cmp_batch
feature_plan.reason: Same CMP batch used across different DAKs may indicate batch-level fraud or data manipulation, especially if the batches are not logically grouped.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_duplicate_distinct_upload_batch
feature_plan.reason: Reused upload batches across different DAKs may indicate data reprocessing or duplication, which can hide fraudulent or duplicate payments.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_duplicate_distinct_ncs_transaction_id
feature_plan.reason: Same NCS transaction ID used across multiple DAKs is a strong indicator of transaction reuse or manipulation, which is a high-risk forensic signal.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_duplicate_distinct_detail_code_head
feature_plan.reason: Same detail_code_head used across different DAKs may indicate a pattern of code reuse, which could be used to mask improper or unauthorized payments.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_rolling_window_duplicate_cmp_batch
feature_plan.reason: Repeated CMP batches within a 7-day window may indicate batch-level manipulation or reuse, which is a red flag for systemic fraud.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_rolling_window_duplicate_upload_batch
feature_plan.reason: Repeated upload batches within a 7-day window may indicate data reprocessing or duplication, which can mask fraudulent or duplicate payments.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_rolling_window_duplicate_ncs_transaction_id
feature_plan.reason: Repeated NCS transaction IDs within a 7-day window suggest transaction reuse or manipulation, which is a serious forensic red flag.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_rolling_window_duplicate_detail_code_head
feature_plan.reason: Repeated detail_code_head values within a 7-day window may indicate a pattern of code reuse, which could be used to mask improper or unauthorized expenditures.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_rolling_window_amount_sum_cmp_batch
feature_plan.reason: High rolling sum of amounts for the same CMP batch within 7 days may indicate batch-level manipulation or reuse, which is a red flag for systemic fraud.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_rolling_window_amount_sum_upload_batch
feature_plan.reason: High rolling sum of amounts for the same upload batch within 7 days may indicate data reprocessing or duplication, which can mask fraudulent or duplicate payments.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_rolling_window_amount_sum_ncs_transaction_id
feature_plan.reason: High rolling sum of amounts for the same NCS transaction ID within 7 days suggests transaction reuse or manipulation, which is a serious forensic red flag.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_rolling_window_amount_sum_detail_code_head
feature_plan.reason: High rolling sum of amounts for the same detail_code_head within 7 days may indicate a pattern of code reuse, which could be used to mask improper or unauthorized expenditures.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_ratio_to_group_median_amount
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount significantly higher than the median amount for its detail_code_head may indicate an outlier, which could be a sign of overpayment or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_ratio_to_group_median_amount_cmp_batch
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount significantly higher than the median for its CMP batch may indicate a large outlier, which could be a sign of overpayment or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_ratio_to_group_median_amount_upload_batch
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount much higher than the median for its upload batch may indicate a large outlier, which could be a sign of overpayment or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_ratio_to_group_median_amount_ncs_transaction_id
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount significantly higher than the median for its NCS transaction ID may indicate a duplicate or inflated transaction, which is a red flag.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_ratio_to_group_median_amount_detail_code_head
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount much higher than the median for its detail_code_head may indicate an outlier, which could be a sign of overpayment or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_group_rank_pct_amount
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount in the top percentile for its detail_code_head may indicate an outlier, which could be a sign of overpayment or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_group_rank_pct_amount_cmp_batch
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount in the top percentile for its CMP batch may indicate a large outlier, which could be a sign of overpayment or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_group_rank_pct_amount_upload_batch
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount in the top percentile for its upload batch may indicate a large outlier, which could be a sign of overpayment or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_group_rank_pct_amount_ncs_transaction_id
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount in the top percentile for its NCS transaction ID may indicate a duplicate or inflated transaction, which is a red flag.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_group_rank_pct_amount_detail_code_head
feature_plan.reason: A payment amount in the top percentile for its detail_code_head may indicate an outlier, which could be a sign of overpayment or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_group_frequency
feature_plan.reason: A detail_code_head used in many payments may indicate a high-volume or high-risk code, which may require closer scrutiny for potential abuse.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_group_frequency_cmp_batch
feature_plan.reason: A CMP batch used in many payments may indicate batch-level manipulation or reuse, which is a red flag for systemic fraud.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_group_frequency_upload_batch
feature_plan.reason: An upload batch used in many payments may indicate data reprocessing or duplication, which can mask fraudulent or duplicate payments.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_group_frequency_ncs_transaction_id
feature_plan.reason: An NCS transaction ID used in many payments may indicate transaction reuse or manipulation, which is a serious forensic red flag.

feature_plan.name: is_payment_with_high_group_frequency_detail_code_head
feature_plan.reason: A detail_code_head used in many payments may indicate a high-volume or high-risk code, which may require closer scrutiny for potential abuse.

why_this_source: schedule3 is the final payment stage in the third-party payment flow; it contains the last recorded payment amount and date. It links directly to cheque_slip and dak, enabling forensic checks on flow integrity, timing, and downstream consistency. Its high cardinality and direct FK relationships make

feature_plan.name: schedule3_flow_broken_upstream
feature_plan.reason: A missing fk_dak indicates the schedule3 record has no link to the originating DAK, breaking the payment flow and suggesting a fabricated or orphaned payment entry.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_flow_broken_cheque_slip
feature_plan.reason: A missing fk_cheque_slip means the schedule3 record is not tied to a valid cheque slip, indicating a payment entry without a verified payment instrument.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_date_in_future
feature_plan.reason: A future dp_sheet_date indicates a payment entry with an invalid or fabricated date, which is a red flag for data manipulation.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_duplicate_dak_payment
feature_plan.reason: Multiple schedule3 records for the same DAK indicate duplicate payment entries, which may suggest double payment or splitting of a single claim.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_duplicate_cheque_slip_payment
feature_plan.reason: Multiple schedule3 records linked to the same cheque slip suggest duplicate finalization of a single payment, which is a strong indicator of duplication.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_duplicate_normalized_cmp_batch
feature_plan.reason: Normalized CMP batch numbers (case-insensitive, punctuation-stripped) appearing multiple times may indicate batch-level manipulation or reuse of payment batches.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_rolling_window_payment_count
feature_plan.reason: A high count of schedule3 entries for the same DAK within 7 days may indicate bill splitting or rapid-fire payments to evade scrutiny.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_rolling_window_payment_amount_sum
feature_plan.reason: A large sum of schedule3 amounts for the same DAK within 7 days may indicate a split payment or overpayment attempt.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_amount_ratio_to_dak_median
feature_plan.reason: If the schedule3 amount is significantly higher than the median amount for the same DAK, it may indicate an outlier payment, possibly due to overpayment or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_amount_rank_pct
feature_plan.reason: A very high percentile rank of the schedule3 amount within the same DAK group may indicate an unusually large payment, warranting further review.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_amount_frequency
feature_plan.reason: High frequency of the same amount for a single DAK may suggest standardized or templated payments, which could be indicative of fraud or automation abuse.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_transcription_type_invalid
feature_plan.reason: A transcription_type of 'X' is invalid and may indicate a corrupted or unprocessed entry, which could be used to hide fraudulent activity.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_month_invalid
feature_plan.reason: Missing month context prevents proper accounting and audit trail alignment, increasing risk of untraceable payments.

feature_plan.name: schedule3_dak_section_invalid
feature_plan.reason: If the DAK is in section 142 (TPP), but the schedule3 record has no fk_section, it breaks the business rule and may indicate a misclassified payment.

why_this_source: ECS is the final payment stage in the third-party payment flow; it contains the last valid payment record with full downstream context. It links to DAK, schedule3, and cheque_slip, enabling forensic checks on flow integrity, approval status, payment timing, and beneficiary alignment. All downstream

feature_plan.name: ecs_flow_broken_without_upstream_dak
feature_plan.reason: A missing fk_dak in ECS indicates a payment record with no valid DAK origin, violating the mandatory DAK-to-ecs flow. This is a critical forensic red flag for fake or orphaned payments.

feature_plan.name: ecs_flow_broken_without_upstream_schedule3
feature_plan.reason: ECS without a linked schedule3 indicates a payment that bypassed the schedule3 stage, which is required for payment validation. This breaks the normal flow and suggests manipulation.

feature_plan.name: ecs_flow_broken_without_upstream_cheque_slip
feature_plan.reason: ECS without a cheque_slip link indicates a payment that skipped the cheque slip stage, which is a required intermediate step. This breaks the payment chain and raises suspicion.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_with_rejected_status
feature_plan.reason: ECS records with record_status=R (rejected) should not be processed further. Their presence in the final payment stage indicates a flow contradiction and potential manipulation.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_with_invalid_payment_status
feature_plan.reason: ECS records with cmp_payment_status=I (invalid) are not eligible for payment. Their presence in the final stage indicates a flow break and potential fraud.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_amount_ratio_to_dak_median
feature_plan.reason: ECS payment amount that deviates significantly from the median amount for the same DAK may indicate duplicate or split payment behavior.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_amount_ratio_to_schedule3_median
feature_plan.reason: ECS payment amount that deviates significantly from the median amount for the same schedule3 may indicate overpayment or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_amount_ratio_to_cheque_slip_median
feature_plan.reason: ECS payment amount that deviates significantly from the median amount for the same cheque_slip may indicate overpayment or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_amount_rank_pct
feature_plan.reason: ECS payment amount that is an extreme percentile within the same DAK group may indicate outlier behavior or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_amount_rank_pct_schedule3
feature_plan.reason: ECS payment amount that is an extreme percentile within the same schedule3 group may indicate outlier behavior or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_amount_rank_pct_cheque_slip
feature_plan.reason: ECS payment amount that is an extreme percentile within the same cheque_slip group may indicate outlier behavior or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_dak
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records for the same DAK may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious in a normal payment flow.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_schedule3
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records for the same schedule3 may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_cheque_slip
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records for the same cheque_slip may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_beneficiary
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records for the same beneficiary may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_payment_reference
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records with the same payment_reference_no may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_cmp_reference
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records with the same cmp_reference_no may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_scroll_no
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records with the same scroll_no may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_utr_no
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records with the same utr_no may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_normalized_utr_no
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records with the same normalized utr_no (ignoring case/punctuation) may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_normalized_cmp_reference
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records with the same normalized cmp_reference_no may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_normalized_payment_reference
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records with the same normalized payment_reference_no may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_normalized_scroll_no
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records with the same normalized scroll_no may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_normalized_beneficiary
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records with the same normalized beneficiary may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_normalized_dak
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records with the same normalized DAK may indicate duplicate payment or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_distinct_beneficiary
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records for the same DAK with different beneficiaries may indicate beneficiary diversion or payment to unauthorized parties.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_distinct_payment_mode
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records for the same DAK with different payment modes may indicate manipulation or inconsistent payment routing.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_duplicate_distinct_bank_pan
feature_plan.reason: Multiple ECS records for the same DAK with different bank account details may indicate payment diversion or manipulation.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_rolling_window_count_7d_same_dak
feature_plan.reason: High count of ECS payments for the same DAK within 7 days may indicate bill splitting or rapid payment processing, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_rolling_window_amount_sum_7d_same_dak
feature_plan.reason: High total amount of ECS payments for the same DAK within 7 days may indicate bill splitting or overpayment, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_rolling_window_count_7d_same_beneficiary
feature_plan.reason: High count of ECS payments to the same beneficiary within 7 days may indicate duplicate or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_rolling_window_amount_sum_7d_same_beneficiary
feature_plan.reason: High total amount of ECS payments to the same beneficiary within 7 days may indicate duplicate or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_rolling_window_count_7d_same_payment_reference
feature_plan.reason: High count of ECS payments with the same payment_reference_no within 7 days may indicate duplicate or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_rolling_window_amount_sum_7d_same_payment_reference
feature_plan.reason: High total amount of ECS payments with the same payment_reference_no within 7 days may indicate duplicate or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_rolling_window_count_7d_same_cmp_reference
feature_plan.reason: High count of ECS payments with the same cmp_reference_no within 7 days may indicate duplicate or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_rolling_window_amount_sum_7d_same_cmp_reference
feature_plan.reason: High total amount of ECS payments with the same cmp_reference_no within 7 days may indicate duplicate or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_rolling_window_count_7d_same_scroll_no
feature_plan.reason: High count of ECS payments with the same scroll_no within 7 days may indicate duplicate or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_rolling_window_amount_sum_7d_same_scroll_no
feature_plan.reason: High total amount of ECS payments with the same scroll_no within 7 days may indicate duplicate or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.name: ecs_payment_rolling_window_count_7d_same_utr_no
feature_plan.reason: High count of ECS payments with the same utr_no within 7 days may indicate duplicate or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.

feature_plan.reason: High total amount of ECS payments with the same utr_no within 7 days may indicate duplicate or split payment behavior, which is suspicious.
